title: cyperus retrofractus
intro: Cyperus retrofractus, also known as Gac or Asian gac, is a type of plant that belongs to the sedge family and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, containing medicinal properties such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Cyperus retrofractus has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried, ground into powder, or made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
